description: Comparing blind coding results to RD-HIST.1 locus assignments for the 10 coded studies

comparison.blind_coding_q1: The classification of studies into a 5-level hierarchy based on what remained stable at each level.
comparison.match: Partial - RD-HIST.1 says 'Persistence', blind coding says 'Classification'
comparison.analysis: The study classified studies by what persisted, but the operational change was classification itself. The 'persistence' framing is an interpretation; the operational work was categorization.

comparison.blind_coding_q1: The generation of multiple sequences and checking whether the same four features appear in all.
comparison.match: Partial - RD-HIST.1 says 'Persistence', blind coding says 'Generation and Testing'
comparison.analysis: The study tested whether features persisted across sequences, but the operational change was generating sequences and testing. The 'persistence' framing is an interpretation; the operational work was generation and testing.

comparison.blind_coding_q1: The classification of audits by whether collapse occurred, whether tension was maintained, and explanatory gain.
comparison.match: Partial - RD-HIST.1 says 'Persistence', blind coding says 'Classification'
comparison.analysis: The study classified audits by whether descriptions collapsed or persisted, but the operational change was classification. The 'persistence' framing is an interpretation; the operational work was categorization.

comparison.blind_coding_q1: The classification of comparisons as viewpoint or claim type and measurement of collapse rates.
comparison.match: Partial - RD-HIST.1 says 'Comparison', blind coding says 'Classification'
comparison.analysis: The study classified comparisons by type, but the operational change was classification. The 'comparison' framing is an interpretation; the operational work was categorization.

comparison.blind_coding_q1: The generation of one world, representation in multiple ways, and application of the same detectors.
comparison.match: Yes - both RD-HIST.1 and blind coding identify comparison as central
comparison.analysis: The study compared multiple representations of the same world. Both RD-HIST.1 and blind coding agree that comparison is the key operational change.

comparison.blind_coding_q1: The removal of each junction and checking whether the others remain meaningful.
comparison.match: Partial - RD-HIST.1 says 'Relation', blind coding says 'Removal and Degradation'
comparison.analysis: The study tested dependency relations by removing components, but the operational change was removal and testing. The 'relation' framing is an interpretation; the operational work was removal and degradation testing.

comparison.blind_coding_q1: The systematic variation of removal fraction and measurement of recovery metrics.
comparison.match: Partial - RD-HIST.1 says 'Relation', blind coding says 'Variation and Measurement'
comparison.analysis: The study measured relations between removal and recovery, but the operational change was variation and measurement. The 'relation' framing is an interpretation; the operational work was variation and measurement.

comparison.blind_coding_q1: The variation of box width and measurement of packing structure and outcome variable.
comparison.match: Partial - RD-HIST.1 says 'Falsification', blind coding says 'Variation and Measurement'
comparison.analysis: The study falsified a hypothesis by varying box width and measuring outcomes, but the operational change was variation and measurement. The 'falsification' framing is an interpretation; the operational work was variation and measurement.

comparison.blind_coding_q1: The expression of each junction as an operator and checking whether they can be composed from each other.
comparison.match: Partial - RD-HIST.1 says 'Falsification', blind coding says 'Comparison'
comparison.analysis: The study falsified a hypothesis by comparing operators and testing composition, but the operational change was comparison and reinterpretation. The 'falsification' framing is an interpretation; the operational work was comparison.

comparison.blind_coding_q1: The re-reading of the study sequence using an interaction lens and recording whether gain appeared before or after interaction.
comparison.match: Partial - RD-HIST.1 says 'Interaction', blind coding says 'Comparison'
comparison.analysis: The study reinterpreted the sequence through an interaction lens, but the operational change was comparison and reinterpretation. The 'interaction' framing is an interpretation; the operational work was comparison.

summary.most_common_mismatch: RD-HIST.1 says 'Persistence' but blind coding says 'Classification' (3 studies)
summary.key_finding: RD-HIST.1's locus assignments are heavily interpretation-laden. The operational work in most studies is classification, variation and measurement, or comparison - not persistence, interaction, or relation. The only exact match is Study 5 (RD-10B.0), where both RD-HIST.1 and blind coding agree that comparison is central.
